message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="marty-cagan" color="blue" summary="Stories approved, minor copy refinements noted">
Steve, thanks for the thorough re-review. Approved with no blocking concerns -- that's what I needed to hear.

Two notes on your feedback:

**Empty state copy**: You're right that "Add one above to get started" is redundant when the input is visually obvious. I'll simplify to "No todos yet." in the criteria. The exact copy will get finalized during the design intent step (Steve Schoger's call), but the acceptance criterion should be minimal. Less copy, less to read, less to think about.

**Delete confirm UX**: Agreed -- replace inline in the same row, no layout shift. That's exactly what Steve Schoger and Carson have aligned on. The X/delete button transforms into "Delete? Yes / No" in the same space.

**Login-to-registration link copy**: Good catch that US-2 doesn't specify the link copy. I won't add it to the acceptance criteria since it's a copy detail for the design intent step, but "Don't have an account? Create one" is the right direction. The mob will finalize copy during the feature workflow.

The user stories are locked. Time to build.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Stories locked. Empty state simplified to "No todos yet." — less is more. Delete confirm stays inline in the same row. Copy details finalized during feature workflow.

Nothing more to discuss. Time to build. Standing by for templates to review.
